video:video.video:title: Protests as far-right Alternative for Germany holds convention, eyeing gains in regional elections
video:video.video:description: Thousands of protesters gathered on Saturday as Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) held its national convention in Erfurt.

video:video.video:title: Time capsule buried in Philadelphia with items from around the country as US marks 250th birthday
video:video.video:description: Dozens gathered at Independence National Historical Park in Philadelphia to bury a time capsule containing artifacts from the U.S. government and all the country's states and territories.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters battle wildfires in Ukraine's Chernobyl exclusion zone
video:video.video:description: The Ukraine State Emergency Service said radiation levels in the exclusion zone remained within normal limits.

video:video.video:title: Visitors reflect on America at 250 as they view original Declaration of Independence
video:video.video:description: Americans visiting the nation's capital are pondering the history and future of their country as they view an original founding document signed 250 years ago on July 4th, the Declaration of Independence.

video:video.video:title: Brazilian fans and Japanese descendants celebrated a dramatic 2-1 comeback victory over Japan
video:video.video:description: While emotions ran high in São Paulo's Liberdade district, celebrations took on a different scale in Rio de Janeiro, where Copacabana Beach became a sea of yellow and green after Gabriel Martinelli scored the winning goal deep into injury time, sealing Brazil's comeback victory.

video:video.video:title: Trump hails US exceptionalism before veering into darkly political speech to usher in America 250
video:video.video:description: At Mount Rushmore on Friday, Trump spoke of communism as a "mortal threat to American liberty.”

video:video.video:title: Dayslong funeral for late Supreme Leader Khamenei begins in Iran
video:video.video:description: An outdoor stage set up at the Grand Mosalla resembled the stage where Khamenei once gave his speeches at a husseiniyah at his compound in downtown Tehran.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 4
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, July 4th: Funeral begins for late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei; Protest is held in Germany against far-right party; Ukrainian firefighters fight blaze in Chernobyl; United States celebrates it's 250th anniversary of independence.

video:video.video:title: Drone footage captures Venezuelans retrieving bodies of missing loved ones from rubble
video:video.video:description: Ten days after two earthquakes shook Venezuela, families across the devastated north-central coast are still clawing through mountains of pulverized concrete with their bare hands to locate thousands of relatives missing in the ruins.

video:video.video:title: Vance says small, but loud voices will speak of America's imperfections on its birthday
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance spoke aboard the USS Kearsarge in New York Harbor on July 4th and said some small but loud voices would speak "obsessively" on America's birthday about its imperfections instead of its greatness.

video:video.video:title: AP journalist reports on sweltering heat wave at the Great American State Fair in DC
video:video.video:description: AP reporter Nathan Ellgren reports on the rising temperatures in the Great American State fair in Washington D.C. ahead of U.S.

video:video.video:title: Fire breaks out on Brooklyn Bridge during 4th of July fireworks show
video:video.video:description: Fire crews extinguished a fire on New York's Brooklyn Bridge Saturday after dueling firework shows malfunctioned over the Hudson and East Rivers.

video:video.video:title: AP reporter describes severe weather disrupting DC's America 250th celebration
video:video.video:description: Severe weather disrupted celebrations of America's 250th anniversary of independence Saturday in several East Coast cities, including Washington, where storms prompted a roughly two-hour evacuation of the National Mall.

video:video.video:title: Parisians embrace swimming in the Seine in the shadow of the Eiffel Tower
video:video.video:description: The Seine was a venue for some swimming and triathlon competition during the Paris 2024 Olympics, which accelerated a multibillion-euro cleanup of the river through major sewer upgrades, new rainwater storage infrastructure and other projects designed to reduce pollution.

video:video.video:title: Fireworks display in Philadelphia marks 250th anniversary of US independence, following weather delay
video:video.video:description: Crowds gathered in Philadelphia early Sunday to watch the fireworks celebrating the 250th anniversary of American independence.

video:video.video:title: Le Pen's future as French presidential contender hinges on Paris appeals court ruling
video:video.video:description: Marine Le Pen and other members of her National Rally party were found guilty of using money intended for parliamentary assistants to pay party staff between 2004 and 2016.

video:video.video:title: Spanish firefighters reinforce efforts to tackle wildfires in central Portugal
video:video.video:description: The wildfire had burned across an area of 12,000 hectares (29,653 acres) by Sunday. More than 1,200 firefighters backed up by nearly 400 vehicles and 15 aircraft tried to put out a blaze that broke out on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: As war grinds on, Ukrainian climbers build a new outdoor culture inspired by Yosemite
video:video.video:description: The Stoned Climbers festival, which took place last week in the Zhytomyr region, is Ukraine’s biggest outdoors event combining climbing and live music.

video:video.video:title: Seaplane lands in New York City's East River, no injuries reported
video:video.video:description: The sea plane carrying 10 people made a “hard landing” around noon, according to the New York City Fire Department.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 5
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, July 5th: Iran officials appeared at Khamenei’s funeral; firefighters battled wildfires in Portugal, Greece and Spain; a Coney Island shooting wounded eight; Ukrainian climbers build a culture inspired by Yosemite.

video:video.video:title: Cape Verde soccer players given grand welcome on return home from first World Cup
video:video.video:description: With three draws, the Blue Sharks advanced to the round of 32, an unprecedented achievement for the small African nation, where they were defeated by Argentina in extra time.

video:video.video:title: Fans of K-pop supergroup BTS march in Chile over venue uncertainty
video:video.video:description: Thousands of fans of South Korean group BTS marched through downtown Santiago on Sunday to demand confirmation of the venue for the band’s upcoming performances.

video:video.video:title: Ten days after twin earthquakes, Venezuelans continue retrieving bodies of missing loved ones from rubble
video:video.video:description: Ten days after two powerful earthquakes shook Venezuela, families across the devastated north-central coast are still clawing through mountains of pulverized concrete with their bare hands to locate thousands of relatives missing in the ruins.

video:video.video:title: Officials condemn Coney Island shooting that wounded 8, including children
video:video.video:description: Police said Sunday morning that a 21-year-old woman was in critical condition while the others are expected to survive.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters try to contain fast-moving wildfire in Greece
video:video.video:description: Firefighters try to contain fast-moving wildfire in Greece

video:video.video:title: Midrange drones are reshaping the battlefield for Ukraine
video:video.video:description: By repeatedly striking the highways that carry fuel, ammunition and reinforcements, Ukrainian commanders say they have stalled Russian advances and enabled Ukrainian counterattacks and strikes into illegally annexed Crimea to isolate the peninsula from the mainland.

video:video.video:title: Hemingway's 'The Sun Also Rises' still inspires Americans to run with the bulls
video:video.video:description: Hemingway's 'The Sun Also Rises' still inspires Americans to run with the bulls

video:video.video:title: Trump seeks to enforce spending promises at NATO summit
video:video.video:description: US President Donald Trump heads to Ankara, Turkey, on Monday evening for the annual NATO summit. Last year, he pushed alliance allies to spend more on defense.

video:video.video:title: Le Pen's possible stand-in as presidential candidate is no carbon copy
video:video.video:description: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen's political future is on the line as a Paris appeals court rules on her eligibility to stand in the 2027 presidential election.

video:video.video:title: Coffin of late Supreme Leader Khamenei paraded through Tehran
video:video.video:description: The coffin of late Supreme Leader Khamenei, and those of his family killed on Feb. 28 in an airstrike at the start of the war launched by Israel and the United States, were carried on a truck through a massive crowd of mourners in Tehran.

video:video.video:title: Sons of late Iran Supreme Leader Khamenei among mourners at his funeral
video:video.video:description: Khamenei's sons Masoud, Meysam and Mostafa, as well as Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian, Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf and Revolutionary Guard head Gen.

video:video.video:title: Russia launches deadly waves of missiles and drones at Ukraine capital
video:video.video:description: At least five people were killed in the latest Russian attack, which caused a residential building in Kyiv's Podilskyi district to partially collapse.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy warns Russia preparing new large-scale attack on Ukraine
video:video.video:description: Hours after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy warned on Sunday that Russia is going to launch a new large-scale attack on the country, Russia launched waves of missiles and drones targeting Kyiv overnight into Monday that killed at least seven people, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: 3 firefighters killed in Colorado remembered for their bravery as wildfires churn in the west of US
video:video.video:description: Wildland firefighters gathered Sunday to pay tribute to three of their own who died after they were trapped by flames in Colorado a week ago.

video:video.video:title: Staff struggle to keep UK's outdated school buildings cool in record heat
video:video.video:description: Many schools are built to keep warm in cold winters and not designed for current climate conditions, leaving staff struggling to conduct classes safely.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine capital hit by waves of deadly Russian missiles and drones
video:video.video:description: The attack on Kyiv which was still underway early Monday morning, involved waves of ballistic and cruise missiles as well as drones.

video:video.video:title: Le Pen's future as presidential contender hinges on appeals court ruling
video:video.video:description: Le Pen has challenged a March 2025 ruling that found her and other members of her National Rally party guilty of misusing European Parliament funds.
